November 3, 2009, the Company issued a press release to announce certain global restructuring initiatives and to confirm its earnings guidance for full-year 2009 of $4.54 - $4.59 per share, which excludes the impact of special items.
Medsystems, Inc., a cardiovascular device company, announced a definitive agreement whereby Johnson & Johnson will acquire Conor Medsystems for approximately $1.4 billion.
Pharmaceutical, Inc., a Johnson & Johnson company, acquired Colbar LifeScience Ltd., a privately held biotechnology company specializing in reconstructive medicine and tissue engineering.

Johnson & Johnson, announced the completion of the acquisition of Vascular Control Systems, Inc., a privately held company focused on developing medical devices to treat fibroids and to control bleeding in obstetric and gynecologic applications.

Development, L.L.C., a Johnson & Johnson company, issued a press release indicating that it is discontinuing its current clinical development program for its controlled release formulation of topiramate in obesity and type 2 diabetes.

Johnson company, issued a press release announcing that it received a subpoena from the Inspector General, Department of Health and Human Services, requesting documents related to the sales and marketing of PROCRITr (Epoetin Alfa).
